description: A workload is bound to a ClusterRole with wildcard verbs/resources or cluster-admin, granting far more than it needs.
body: Failure mode: a ServiceAccount is bound to cluster-admin or a ClusterRole using verbs:['*'] and resources:['*'], so a compromised pod can read all secrets, modify any object, or escalate across namespaces. Detection signals: kubectl auth can-i --list shows broad or wildcard access; bindings reference cluster-admin; the SA token can list secrets cluster-wide. Do not use wildcards or cluster-admin to make a workload function. Safer fallback: replace with a namespaced Role enumerating exact apiGroups/resources/verbs, bind the dedicated SA, and verify can-i returns 'no' for everything outside the workload's needs.
